title: AI Operations Manager
url_note: Direct per-job Workable code not extractable from JS-rendered board; this is Fuse Energy's own Workable ATS board where the live AI Operations Manager posting appears. BuiltIn London mirror (reposted 7 days ago) confirms role active.
requirements_met: Identify opportunities for AI implementation across business: matches Ryan building AI agents/automations
Redesign operational processes with AI: ops generalist + AI tooling
Construct internal AI tools to boost efficiency: hands-on AI builder
Drive automation and decision-making across workflows: operator background
gaps: Trading/energy-network domain context may need ramp-up
preferred_met: Comfort turning AI from tools into operational backbone
Cross-functional operations span (trading, networks, internal workflows)

experience_fit.calculation: Manager title but operator-IC scope; mid-level builder fits AI-ops mandate

industry_match.calculation: Energy tech with AI as operational backbone - strong AI tooling, energy domain adjacency

location_match.calculation: London in-office; Ryan covers London (Cambridge friction)

salary_penalty.reason: Salary not listed; well-funded startup, role likely above GBP 50k floor

notes: Fuse Energy AI Operations Manager: build internal AI tools, redesign processes, deploy AI across trading/energy networks/internal workflows. Distinct role from CEO-office FA at same company. Confirmed active via Built In London.

must_haves.note: Ryan holds a non-technical BA Business Management (2:1, Leeds) — explicit STEM requirement not met
Self-directed AI-agent building with Claude Code shows applied coding fluency, but no formal software-engineering / professional programming background

must_haves.req: Identify high-impact AI opportunities & redesign processes to be AI-first
must_haves.note: Operator who builds AI agents/automations; strong process-redesign instinct

must_haves.req: Build internal AI tools to supercharge teams
must_haves.note: Hands-on AI-agent builder for growth/intel use cases
Alexa failure analysis, growth experiments, ambiguity navigation

hard_blockers: Explicit STEM degree requirement — Ryan's degree is non-technical Business Management
Coding experience stated as a requirement; Ryan's is applied/self-taught (Claude Code) not professional SWE — likely below their bar given STEM framing
met_strengths: Genuine AI-agent-builder fit for the 'AI as operational backbone' mandate
interview_match.one_line_verdict: Strong on the AI-builder mandate but the explicit STEM-degree + coding-experience requirements are a screening gate Ryan doesn't formally clear — stretch unless they weight applied AI-agent work over credentials.
